summary: Frontend-only verification of the /amenities page divider fix. All 3 category sections render (Lifestyle, Convenience, Infrastructure & Reliability) with 5 amenity cards each (15 total). Layout is 3+2 at 1920px desktop. Each amenity card has a 1px non-transparent border-right (rgb(234, 229, 219) brand-beige) and border-bottom, which produces the divider between EVERY adjacent pair of cards INCLUDING the two bottom-row cards. The previously missing middle vertical divider between bottom-row cards is now visibly present (confirmed both by computed styles and by screenshot). No console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: AmenitiesPage.jsx is clean and uses data-testid for cards/categories. Per-card border-r + border-b combined with container border-l + border-t is the correct and idiomatic approach to render dividers between every pair of cells in a wrap-based grid; this fix is robust to any item count (not only 3+2).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified at desktop viewport 1920x1080. Bottom-row cards in every category sit at the same y, share x=960.5 boundary (card 3 right edge == card 4 left x), and each card has border-right: 1px solid rgb(234,229,219). Layout confirms 3+2 wrap at lg breakpoint. Screenshot saved to .screenshots/amenities_grid.png shows the divider clearly between the two bottom-row cards. No console errors detected on the page.
rca of the issue: Original bug: a flex `gap-px` divider approach only rendered dividers at the gaps between cards in a single row; on a 3+2 wrap, the two bottom cards filled the row with no intermediate gap-divider since the flex gap only inserts between adjacent siblings in the same line and the divider technique relied on background-color showing through the gap, which the bottom 2 stretched cards covered. Fix: switched to per-card `border-r border-b` on each card + `border-l border-t` on the container. Because every card draws its own right+bottom border, a 1px line appears between any two adjacent cards in any row count, including the bottom-row middle. Verified rendered output.
